pi_items.verified_value: Grunfeld, Y. (1958). 'The Determinants of Corporate Investment.' Unpublished Ph.D. Dissertation, University of Chicago. Published in econometrics references including Greene (2003, 2012) Table F13.1
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm Grunfeld's 1958 dissertation is the original source; Greene's textbooks contain this data in appendices
Data embedded in setup_task.sh shows startobs=1935 and endobs=1954 with 20 observations
Verified by matching embedded data values with known Grunfeld dataset; confirmed in variables: invest_ge, value_ge, capital_ge, invest_wh, value_wh, capital_wh

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the authentic Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset containing 20 annual observations (1935-1954) for General Electric and Westinghouse, containing six variables: investment, market value, and capital stock for each firm. The data is embedded in the setup script and matches known published values. The expected SUR coefficient ranges are based on actual published SUR results from SAS/ETS documentation: General Electric value coefficient is 0.038310 (claimed range 0.02-0.06), capital coefficient is 0.139036 (claimed range 0.05-0.25); Westinghouse value coefficient is 0.057630 (claimed range 0.03-0.09), capital coefficient is 0.063978 (claimed range 0.01-0.20). The cross-equation correlation between GE and WH equations in SUR estimation is 0.72896.
